Kousar Respondents Province of Sindh and others C.P.No.D- 414 of 2023 Petitioner Rakhi Respondents Province of Sindh and others C.P.No.D- 415 of 2023 Petitioner Naina Arzoo Respondents Province of Sindh and others C.P.No.D- 416 of 2023 Petitioner Robina Bibi Respondents Province of Sindh and others C.P.No.D- 417 of 2023 Petitioner Inayat Masih Respondents Province of Sindh and others C.P.No.D- 7629 of 2022 Petitioner Mst. Mai Farhat Khatoon Respondents Province of Sindh and others Date of Hearing: 09.05.2023 Date of Judgment: 09.05.2023 JUDGMENT NAIMATULLAH PHULPOTO, J.- By this common judgment, we intend to decide the captioned petitions as common question of law and facts is involved in the petitions. The main grievance of the petitioners is that they have been deprived of pensionary benefits by the respondents/ KMC/DMCs. Counsel appearing for the KMC/DMCs are shifting burden of the payment of pensionary benefits from one to another. Neither KMC nor DMCs take the responsibility to pay the pensionary benefits to the petitioners. Resultantly, petitioners have been deprived of pensionary benefits. 2. On the last date of hearing, Secretary Local Government, who is administrative head of Municipal Corporations was directed to appear before this Court and file his specific reply mentioning therein about the responsibility of KMC or DMCs to pay the pensionary benefits to the petitioners. Secretary Local Government appeared before this Court, he could not satisfy the Court whether pensionary dues of the petitioners shall be paid by the KMC or DMCs.

On the last date of hearing, Secretary Local Government, who is administrative head of Municipal Corporations was directed to appear before this Court and file his specific reply mentioning therein about the responsibility of KMC or DMCs to pay the pensionary benefits to the petitioners. Secretary Local Government appeared before this Court, he could not satisfy the Court whether pensionary dues of the petitioners shall be paid by the KMC or DMCs. This court cannot grant further time to the respondents to add miseries of petitioners. 3. We have heard learned counsel for the parties and perused the judgment passed by Supreme Court in the case of Haji Muhammad Ismail Memon advocate, complainant (PLD 2007 SC 35), in which it is held as under: “7. It is pathetic condition that Government servants, after having served for a considerable long period during which they give their blood and sweat to the department had to die in a miserable condition on account of non payment of pension/ pensionary benefits etc. The responsibility, of course, can be fixed upon the persons who were directly responsible for the same but at the same time we are of the opinion that it is an overall problem mostly in every department, where public functionaries failed to play their due role even in accordance with law. Resultantly, good governance is suffering badly. Thus everyone who is responsible in any manner in delaying the case of such retired officers/official or widows or orphan children for the recovery of pension/gratuity and G.P. Fund has to be penalized. As their such lethargic action is in violation of Articles 9 and 14 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973. Admittedly, it is against the dignity of a human being that he has to die in miserable condition and for about three years no action has been taken by the concerned quarters in finalizing the pension case and now when the matter came up before the Court, for the first time, they are moving in different directions just to show their efficiency and to clear their position before the Court.

As their such lethargic action is in violation of Articles 9 and 14 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973. Admittedly, it is against the dignity of a human being that he has to die in miserable condition and for about three years no action has been taken by the concerned quarters in finalizing the pension case and now when the matter came up before the Court, for the first time, they are moving in different directions just to show their efficiency and to clear their position before the Court. Such conduct on their behalf is highly condemnable and cannot be encouraged in any manner.” 4. In view of above and while relying upon above cited authority, captioned Constitution Petitions are disposed of in the following terms: (i) Secretary Local Government Sindh shall determine the responsibility of KMC/DMCs in each. (ii) Secretary Local Government shall ensure that pensionary benefits are paid by concerned DMC or KMC to concerned petitioners, if due as per rules. (iii) Chief Secretary Sindh shall ensure compliance of the Court order. 5. Compliance report shall be submitted to this court after one month. In case of non-compliance, Secretary Local Government shall appear before this Court for facing consequences as provided by law. Copy of the judgment shall be sent to the Chief Secretary, Government of Sindh with direction to ensure strict compliance of the Court order. JUDGE JUDGE
